title: FIFA WC: Ronaldo scripts history in Portugal's dominant 5-0 victory over Uzbekistan
content: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Portugal crushed Uzbekistan 5-0 in Houston to strengthen their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout stages. The captain opened the scoring before adding a record-breaking second goal, becoming the first player to score in six consecutive World Cups and surpassing Eusébio as Portugal’s all-time leading World Cup scorer.

title: 'She pushed him, then blamed a snake': Pune man's father claims fiancée's first murder bid failed
content: A Pune businessman, initially thought to have died in a trekking accident, was allegedly murdered by his fiancée and her lover. Police arrested Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary for pushing Ketan Agarwal into a gorge at Lohagad Fort. The couple was set to marry in November. Investigations revealed Siya was in a relationship with Chaudhary and considered Ketan an obstacle.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership
content: Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions.  These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13% drop in its workforce.  The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers.  This move comes amidst broader concerns about AI's impact on tech jobs.

title: How proposed US citizenship fee hike will impact applicants; Indians among hardest hit
content: US Citizenship and Immigration Services plans a significant hike in naturalisation application fees, potentially costing Indian immigrants much more to become American citizens.  The proposed increase for the primary application form could reach $1,330, with reduced-fee options and waivers being eliminated.  This comes as Indians remain a major group seeking US citizenship.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: Iran rushes to sell oil to India after Trump sanctions waiver - all you need to know
content: Data from Vortexa, combined with Bloomberg calculations, show that around 68 million barrels of crude and condensate were floating at sea as of June 22. More than 80% of that volume does not appear to have a confirmed destination, potentially making it available for sale.

title: When breath turned pungent mid-sleep: Ammonia leak survivors recount ordeal
content: A devastating ammonia leak at a seafood processing factory in Tiruvallur district left workers, many young women far from home, in critical condition. The gas, originating from the processing area, seeped into sleeping quarters, incapacitating those off-duty. Emergency services faced delays, with many suffering severe respiratory distress and other ailments. Volunteers and authorities responded, but the incident highlights the vulnerability of workers in such facilities.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: Watch: IShowSpeed loses it as Cristiano Ronaldo scores his first goal of the 2026 World Cup
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's first goal at the 2026 FIFA World Cup ignited an explosive reaction from superfan IShowSpeed, whose stadium celebration went viral.  The streamer's ecstatic display, fueled by his long-standing admiration for the Portuguese star, nearly overshadowed Ronaldo's brace against Uzbekistan. This moment highlights the increasing synergy between online personalities and major sporting events, captivating global audiences.

title: Tom Brady sends clear message after Gisele’s alleged Father’s Day snub
content: Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bundchen, has continued to dominate headlines even after years of their divorce. The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bundchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are.

title: Shedeur Sanders draws trade interest as Browns weigh year 2 quarterback plans
content: ESPN Cleveland radio host Tony Rizzo suggests a shake-up for the Cleveland Browns’ quarterback room. He hints that not all four signal-callers, including Shedeur Sanders, might be present for upcoming training camp. Trade rumors are circulating around Sanders. And insiders question his long-term fit. This follows strong OTA performance from Sanders where he even outshone Deshaun Watson in some eyes. Potential landing spots for Sanders are Cardinals, Buccaneers, and more.

title: New details reveal how Jorge Messi is recovering after a difficult period
content: There is encouraging news for Lionel Messi and his family. Argentine outlet Paparazzi reports that Jorge Messi’s condition has improved and he could leave hospital soon. The update comes after Messi’s emotional World Cup moment brought attention to his father’s health struggles. Meanwhile, the Argentina captain continues to make history on the field with another record-breaking World Cup performance.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for June 24, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 24, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Unconverted: Usha Vance stays firm on Hindu faith as JD Vance praises Pakistan
content: Vice President JD Vance's recent praise for Pakistan has ruffled feathers within the MAGA base, drawing criticism from fellow Republicans. Simultaneously, his wife, Usha Vance, publicly stated her contentment with her Hindu upbringing, sparking discussions about their interfaith marriage and her independent stance. These events are being closely watched as Vance eyes a potential 2028 presidential run.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo rewrites football history, becomes first player to...
content: Cristiano Ronaldo has etched his name in history, becoming the first footballer to score in six FIFA World Cup editions. The 41-year-old captain achieved this remarkable feat against Uzbekistan, extending a record that began in 2006. This milestone comes as Portugal seeks to rebound from a draw, with Ronaldo silencing critics by delivering on the grandest stage once again. His consistent brilliance across two decades solidifies his legacy.

title: Dhurandhars or traitors: How Shinde’s 'Op Tiger' changed Maharashtra’s power dynamics
content: Maharashtra's political scene is rapidly changing, as the recent defection of six Uddhav Sena MPs to Eknath Shinde's faction under 'Operation Tiger' highlights. This trend reflects a broader pattern seen in Trinamool Congress and AAP, where the anti-defection law is being sidestepped, and parties blatantly promote shifts in loyalty. The fear of the Samajwadi Party and the NCP being the next targets in this game of defections shows the vulnerability of regional parties in this political maneuvering.

title: Diabetes cure finally found? First drug treating type 1 diabetes made available
content: In a landmark announcement, the NHS has introduced a beacon of hope for individuals susceptible to type 1 diabetes. Teplizumab, an innovative medication, is now able to delay the disease's onset for an average of three years, focusing on the autoimmune process impacting the pancreas. This breakthrough provides vital time for young patients, allowing them to manage their health more effectively before the disease fully manifests.

title: India survive late Pakistan scare to seal thrilling 4-3 Pro League win
content: India's men's hockey team continued their dominance over Pakistan, securing a thrilling 4-3 victory in the FIH Pro League. Despite Pakistan scoring first, India responded with four goals.  While Pakistan fought back, India's clinical finishing in the circle proved decisive. This win extends India's unbeaten streak against their rivals, though concerns remain over penalty corner conversion and conceding late opportunities.

title: How hydration breaks are reshaping matches at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
content: FIFA’s mandatory hydration breaks at the World Cup, under the guise of player welfare, have sparked debate. While introduced for all matches regardless of weather and conditions, coaches and players argue they disrupt momentum and alter football’s rhythm. Beyond altering the game, the breaks also create significant advertising inventory for broadcasters with Fox Sports in the USA expected to rake in $250 million. At the end of the day, do the commercial interests outweigh the sporting considerations?

title: Who has suffered the biggest pay cut in IPL history as Pant joins DC?
content: Rishabh Pant's return to Delhi Capitals after a disappointing two-year stint with Lucknow Super Giants came with a Rs 12 crore pay cut, from Rs 27 crore to Rs 15 crore. His move highlights how IPL valuations can change dramatically. Other stars who suffered major pay drops include Venkatesh Iyer, Sam Curran, Mitchell Starc, Kyle Jamieson and Jhye Richardson.

title: Vijay's mother recalls how he became 'quieter' after his sister's death
content: Vijay, the newly appointed Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu and a renowned actor, is often seen as the archetype of the protective brother on-screen. This character resonance stems from a profound personal loss—the death of his younger sister at just two years old. His mother shared that this tragedy shaped Vijay's personality, making him quieter and more introspective.

title: 'Attempt to deflect': India snubs Khawaja Asif's 'will go to war over Indus' remark
content: MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal also spoke on the ongoing protest in the Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK), saying the agitation was a  "direct consequence" of "systemic economic exploitation, denial of fundamental rights and administrative oppression in areas under its illegal and forcible occupation".​​ Earlier, Khawaja Asif had issued a warning to India, saying Islamabad could resort to military action if its water security comes under threat.

title: Deadly heat grips France as crowds flock to water, drowning toll mounts to 40
content: The prolonged spell of extreme heat has disrupted daily life across the country. Schools, rail services and sporting events continue to face challenges, particularly in a nation where air conditioning is not widely used. Scientists have repeatedly linked the increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events to human-driven climate change.

title: Ketan Agrawal murder: Birthday plot, secret lover and a detail that exposed fiancee's story
content: A tragic trek to Lohagad Fort has culminated in a murder investigation, with police arresting a woman and her partner for allegedly pushing her fiancé, Ketan Agrawal, to his death. The couple reportedly conspired to eliminate Agrawal as the fiancée was unwilling to marry him. Initially ruled an accident, the skilled trekker's family suspected foul play, leading to the unraveling of the shocking plot.

title: India–US trade deal may be signed before July 24: What’s done and remaining for agreement
content: India and the US are nearing a trade deal, with negotiators meeting in New Delhi to finalize an interim pact before July 24. Discussions aim to recalibrate the agreement following recent US tariff policy shifts. Both nations are pushing for a fair and reciprocal deal that benefits American exporters and supports India's growth, potentially involving significant Indian purchases from the US.

title: Message seen on WhatsApp: Kunal Shah gets real CRED from Meta
content: Kunal Shah is an interesting choice, because WhatsApp does not need a leader to teach people how to send a message; it needs one who understands how trust, status, habit, and small transactions mutate inside closed networks, which is, almost exactly, the discipline CRED was built to practise.

title: 'His body is fragile': Ex-India star raises concern over Hardik Pandya's 2027 WC chances
content: Former India cricketer Mohammad Kaif expressed concern over Hardik Pandya's persistent fitness issues, highlighting a lack of transparency regarding his injuries. Kaif questioned Pandya's long-term availability for future tournaments, including the 2027 ODI World Cup.  Meanwhile, Kaif supported the selectors' decision to pick Ishan Kishan over Yashasvi Jaiswal for the England ODI series, citing Kishan's versatility and current form.

title: Meet Kunal Shah: From delivery boy at 15 to WhatsApp’s global head
content: Kunal Shah, the visionary behind CRED, is set to lead WhatsApp globally, marking a significant milestone for Indian tech talent.  His journey from humble beginnings in Mumbai, marked by early entrepreneurship and a unique approach to education, highlights a remarkable rise.  Shah's success underscores the power of merit and innovation in the competitive tech landscape, offering inspiration to aspiring professionals worldwide.

title: Ukraine's 'army of witches' helping hunt Russian troops in occupied lands — Report
content: The intelligence collected by these operatives has become a key component of Ukraine's so-called "middle-strike" campaign, a sustained drone offensive targeting Russian command posts, logistics hubs, air-defence systems and troop concentrations behind the front line. Resistance members identify locations, verify coordinates and relay information through encrypted channels before Ukrainian forces launch strikes.

title: Henry David Thoreau’s quote on self-belief: A timeless lesson in shaping destiny
content: Henry David Thoreau, a 19th-century philosopher and naturalist, championed self-reliance and living by one's own values. His time at Walden Pond inspired his seminal work, urging readers to simplify life and connect with nature. Thoreau's belief that self-perception shapes destiny, as highlighted in his quote, remains profoundly relevant, reminding us that inner attitudes often dictate our path more than external circumstances.

title: The $750 billion question: Report claims China fudged trade data to fool the IMF
content: According to the Council on Foreign Relations, the comparison relies on incomplete data and fails to capture the sharp rise in China's surplus since late 2024. It notes that China's reported current account surplus increased from about $400 billion to roughly $750 billion, while net exports contributed more than 1.5 percentage points to economic growth. During the same period, the eurozone's surplus fell from around 420 billion euros to 280 billion euros.

title: US Senate Votes To Halt Iran War In Rebuke To Trump
content: The Senate voted 50-48 in favor of the concurrent resolution, which passed the House of Representatives early this month, reflecting growing concern even among some of Trump's Republicans about the unpopular conflict that began on February 28.

title: Alibaba Sues US For Being Linked To Chinese Military
content: Alibaba, the Chinese technology and e-commerce giant, sued the US government on Tuesday over being placed on a list of businesses from China that the Department of Defense linked to that country's military.

title: "Purposefully Done": Trump Says 6 Arrested For Damaging Reflecting Pool
content: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday that six people have been arrested over damage to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.

title: Iranian President Meets Pakistan's Top Leaders, Discusses Peace Initiatives
content: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Tuesday met with Pakistan's top civilian and military leadership and discussed regional developments and peace initiatives involving the US.

title: Cliffs, 'Falls', And Lovers: Fort Murder's Parallels With Meghalaya Killing
content: Separated by time and hundreds of kilometres, the deaths of Raja Raghuvanshi and Ketan Agarwal both cases are bound by one similarity -- they both were killed by women they were planning to spend their lives with.

title: US Envoy Underscores Positive Ties With India, Highlights Tamil Nadu's Role
content: Sergio Gor underscored Washington's commitment to strengthening cooperation with India and suggested that US President Donald Trump attached significant importance to the relationship.

title: Trump Claims Iran Agreed To Full Nuclear Inspections, Tehran Says Otherwise
content: Washington has been negotiating with Tehran on critical issues such as the fate of the Islamic Republic's nuclear program in the wake of a deal the two foes signed aimed at ending the Middle East war.

title: Rubio Says US Will Not Accept Iranian Tolls On Strait Of Hormuz
content: The US and Iran have signed a preliminary deal to halt the war, and concluded a first round of talks in Switzerland.

title: 'Moving As One Like Jellyfish': US Pilot's Encounter With Iran's Drone Swarm
content: According to a report by CNN, the F-15 pilot described seeing multiple Iranian drones moving together in a formation that looked like a "jellyfish". The account was shared during an intelligence debrief after his rescue but has not been publicly reported earlier.

title: Israel Smuggled Starlink Devices Into Iran To Aid Protesters: Former PM
content: Iran has previously accused Israel and the United States of smuggling in the devices to undermine its security. Starlink is not licensed to operate in Iran, but Musk has previously said the service is active there.

title: 40 Drown Across France As It Sees Its Hottest Day Ever
content: France experienced its hottest day on record on Tuesday, a Meteo France forecaster said, with a peak of 44.3 degrees Celsius (111.74 degrees Fahrenheit) in one town in the southwest.

title: Ajit Doval Says Stable India-China Relations Key To Deeper Trust
content: National Security Advisor (NSA) Ajit Doval, during his meeting with the visiting Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi, underlined that stable, predictable and constructive bilateral relations will contribute to enhanced trust and deeper understanding between the two sides.

title: Kingpin In Fake Arms Licence Racket, 8 Others Arrested In Uttarakhand
content: A criminal case was lodged at the Kashipur police station earlier this month. The investigation has already led to the arrest of nine people and the registration of three separate criminal cases.

title: Weather Highlights: Delhi Records Maximum Temperature Of 38.4 Degrees, Air Quality Deteriorates To 'Poor'
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain Alert Highlights: Delhi records max temperature of 38.4C, slightly below average, with poor air quality reported.

title: 3 Injured In Stampede Outside Stadium In Ranchi
content: At least three persons were injured on Tuesday after a stampede outside the JSCA stadium here, where a cricket match was on, police said.

title: Fuel Ready, But Not Pumps: The E100 Challenge
content: Ethanol production capacity has expanded to nearly 2,000 crore litres annually, exceeding the requirements of the E20 programme.

title: Delhi Unveils Health Scheme For 2.7 Lakh Construction Workers
content: The Delhi government has decided to launch a 'Delhi Building and Construction Workers Health Scheme' for the health protection of 2.7 lakh registered building and construction workers and their families, Chief Minister Rekha Gupta said on Tuesday.

title: Qatar Emir Calls PM Modi, Condoles Deaths Of 12 Indians In Blast
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Tuesday received a phone call from the Emir of Qatar, Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, who expressed grief over the death of Indian nationals in a tragic accident at Ras Laffan Industrial City in that country.

title: Police Dog Mary Helps Crack Case Of 6-Year-Old Girl's Sexual Assault In UP
content: When the investigation into the alleged sexual assault of a six-year-old girl appeared to have hit a dead end, a seven-year-old labrador named Mary helped police identify the accused and get him arrested, officials said on Tuesday.

title: Delhi Student To NASA Astronaut Ready For Launch: Anil Menon's Journey
content: How a winter spent vaccinating children in India grounded NASA Astronaut Anil Menon, who is about to live among the stars.

title: Indian Navy warships INS Udaygiri, INS Kavaratti in Vietnam to boost maritime cooperation
content: INS Udaygiri, a stealth frigate, and INS Kavaratti, an anti-submarine warfare corvette, are part of the Eastern Fleet and are led by Rear Admiral Alok Ananda, Flag Officer Commanding Eastern Fleet

title: Ayodhya Ram temple donation case: SIT submits preliminary report to U.P. government
content: The Uttar Pradesh government had on June 13 constituted the SIT on a request from the temple trust, following allegations of misappropriation of donations received at the Ayodhya Ram temple

title: Claims about sugarcane juice directly mixed with petrol, car engine absorbing water because of ethanol “misleading, unsubstantiated”: Petroleum Ministry
content: The ministry sought to emphasise that modern vehicles are equipped with design features that prevent entry of water into fuel tanks.

title: West Bengal Govt to bring in law to auction properties of those involved in corruption, says Suvendu
content: Chief Minister  Suvendu Adhikari said that after the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) came to power about 10,000 Bangladeshi nationals have left the State and 1800 are kept in holding centres of the State government across 12 districts.

title: Wang Yi calls for India, China to accelerate resumption of stalled dialogue mechanisms
content: A lengthier Chinese readout of the talks between NSA Ajit Doval and China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi said that the Chinese leader noted that “India is an important neighbour of China”

title: Nihang standoff in Uttarakhand Gurudwara resolved
content: Rudraprayag District Magistrate Vishal Mishra said the matter was resolved through coordinated efforts by the gurdwara administration and the district authorities

title: After the split, mudslinging begins between the newly inducted Shiv Sena MPs and the Shiv Sena(UBT)
content: Sanjay Raut questioned Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis regarding the law and order situation in the State, and dared Sanjay Dina Patil to face him without his police security cover

title: Stretching ‘information’ to include Telegram
content: By reading entire platforms run by companies, which were legal entities by their own nature, under the ambit of ‘information’ under the Act, the government has effectively weaponised the content-blocking provision of Section 69A

title: Omar Abdullah meets Shashi Tharoor-led Parliamentary committee, calls for sustained engagement to understand J&K’s aspirations
content: It comes days after Mr. Tharoor’s remarks on return of normalcy in J&K evoked sharp reactions both from ruling National Conference and the Congress leaders

title: AAP MLA Chaitar Vasava, wife among nine sentenced to seven years in assault, extortion case
content: Describing the conviction as politically motivated, AAP Gujarat president Isudan Gadhvi said the party would challenge the verdict before a higher court

title: Farmers’ group flags ration risk for lakhs in West Bengal
content: Supreme Court says petitioners must ideally move Calcutta High Court; plea claims that the outcome of SIR has been linked to food security schemes in the State and could impact welfare benefits

title: TVK government is clear on political opposition to and administrative cooperation with the Centre, says Vijay
content: He reaffirms his party remains ideologically opposed to the ruling party at the Centre, while making it clear that it would not oppose the Centre blindly

title: India staring at the biggest opportunity for value addition in food: former CMD, Nestle India
content: The State alone has more than 3,500 millet manufacturing and processing units, making it one of the strongest millet-processing ecosystems in the country, says KAPPEC

title: PM Modi speaks to Qatar’s Amir after 12 Indians killed in Ras Laffan blast, thanks him for condolences
content: Twelve Indians were among 13 people killed in an explosion at an LNG facility in the Ras Laffan Industrial City

title: No bar for granting leave to convicts pending appeals against their conviction, orders five-judge Larger Bench of Madras High Court
content: Passes the interim order pending final determination of a connected case before the Supreme Court and also the reference made to the Larger Bench by a Division Bench of two judges

title: BRICS can play a key role in tackling terrorism: PM
content: ‘India’s chairmanship of BRICS will help in enhancing priorities of the Global South and contribute to a safer world’

title: CM Vijay tells ‘short story’ in Assembly; Udhayanidhi says House turned into a cinema
content: Though the CM did not name anyone from the Opposition parties in his story, his comments were widely interpreted as a reference to the defeat of former CM and DMK president M.K. Stalin in Kolathur

title: Watch: HL: Lucknow building fire: FIR cites illegal commercial use | Above the Fold | 23.06.2026
content: In this episode, we look at the devastating Lucknow building fire and the broader questions it raises about fire safety in India’s cities. We also examine the Centre’s latest FCRA amendments, Meta’s elevation of CRED founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, and more.

title: Will launch fast from July 5 if amendments to RTI Rules not revoked: Anna Hazare
content: Mr. Hazare objected to the hike in fees, saying no rational explanation or financial analysis was provided

title: One-gram gold ring for babies born in T.N. government hospitals
content: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay will inaugurate the initiative on September 15, the birth anniversary of former Chief Minister C.N. Annadurai

title: GMC Commissioner begins two-day Indore visit to study urban governance, sanitation practices
content: Mayur Ashok reviews the implementation of Swachh Bharat Mission, sanitation management systems, door-to-door waste collection mechanisms, scientific processing of solid waste, resource recovery initiatives and circular economy practices

title: Ladakh observes shutdown over Centre’s ‘missing’ draft, new policy push
content: ‘The Centre is making an attempt to dilute the commitments,’ says Tsering Dorjey Lakrook, president of the Ladakh Buddhist Association, among the main groups engaging with the Centre over long-pending demands of Statehood, and inclusion in the Sixth Schedule

title: Adani opens Mundra Airport for scheduled commercial flights to 4 cities
content: With this, Mundra has become the 9th airport of the Adani Group, and this location is set to become a fully integrated, multi-modal logistics and business hub

title: Chhattisgarh man held for killing eight fellow villagers spanning four months
content: Ram Sahay Jaiswal, the accused, had allegedly served poison-laced alcoholic drinks to all the victims with a distinct motive for each crime from February to June this year, according to police

title: Facts related to Syama Prasad Mookerjee’s death in J&K should be made public: BJP
content: The party pays tributes to the sacrifices and vision of Mookerjee and highlights his “enduring legacy in strengthening national unity”

title: New ROB, fruit pulp factory, desilting of water bodies in Tirupattur required: IUML MLA
content: MLA S.S.P. Syed Farooq Basha highlighted these needs during the motion of thanks to Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar’s address in the State Legislative Assembly

title: State government recommends MSP for Totapuri mangoes, awaits Centre’s nod
content: Farmers have demanded to provide ₹8000 to 10,000 per tonne for Totapuri variety mango instead of ₹2000 to 3000 per tonne that is provided every year leaving them under loss

title: School students allegedly roped in by TVK functionaries tie party flags to median
content: The police said that a group of students was on their way to school when they were told by local TVK functionaries to tie party flags with small poles attached to it on a steel median

title: Arms loot: Manipur may go soft on Arambai Tenggol members
content: Govindas Konthoujam, the State’s Home Minister, says the government is trying to avoid arresting members of the radical group while continuing efforts to recover illegally held firearms

title: Operation Toofan: Law will apply equally to film personalities and VIPs in drug cases, says Ramesh Chennithala
content: Referring to alleged prevalence of drug use at DJ parties, luxury hotels, and film-related gatherings, Home Minister says there will be strong action

title: Kerala’s first sci-fi adventure play heads to Thrissur
content: Adapted from Ross Welford’s novel, The Kid Who Came From Space, and directed by Vishnu Prasad, the play explores the idea of coexistence through the friendship between humans and an alien

title: Iran-U.S. MoU has had a positive impact on energy, fertiliser flow to India: MEA
content: MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal says 11 India-bound vessels have transited the Strait of Hormuz since the June 17 signing of Iran-U.S. MoU. He indicates that there has been a positive development in shipping in the past few days

title: Reject 2021 election petition as tenure of 16th Assembly has ended, Duraimurugan urges Madras HC
content: His counsel tells Justice G.K. Ianthiraiyan that election petitions can be decided even after the expiry of the term only if they had been filed on the ground of alleged electoral corrupt practice and not otherwise

title: Former scribe of The Hindu remembered
content: Transport Minister C.P. John describes former Chief of Bureau of The Hindu, S. Anil Radhakrishnan, as emotionally neutral and factually strong

title: Tamil Nadu exercised over the Centre’s guidelines on projects in the Cauvery basin
content: The Centre’s norms assume relevance in the light of the ongoing controversy over the Mekedatu drinking water-cum-balancing reservoir project, mooted by Karnataka

title: Women in A.P. will get free travel in AC buses soon, says Transport Minister
content: Government has prepared a comprehensive action plan to make 2026–27 a milestone year for technology, sustainability and expansion of public services in the transport sector, asserts Mandipalli Ramprasad Reddy; Special Chief Secretary rules out privatisation, allays fears of employees and labour unions

title: Poland donor's stem cells save Kashmir boy suffering from rare immune condition
content: the stem cell donor was from Poland . the transplant was carried out by doctors at sher-i-Kashmir Institute of Medical Sciences (SKIMS) in Srinagar . a three-year-old boy was suffering from Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis .

title: Bank of India wins Rs 100 crore case against Nirav Modi
content: fugitive diamond businessman owes public sector bank over $10.7 million (a little over Rs 100 crore) bank of india had lent one of Nirav’s companies, firestar diamond FZE in 2012 .

title: Ladakh bandh to protest ‘breach’ of agreement in Delhi talks
content: businesses across leh and Kargil remained closed as large numbers of people gathered to hear functionaries of the region's two main political groups . the groups have alleged that draft minutes of the meeting shown to LAB co-chairman Chering Dorjay Lakrook omitted an agreement on a proposed legislative body with authority over the bureaucracy . LAB and KDA have warned that if the “two essentials” are not reflected in the final minutes, they would return to their original demand

title: After annus horribilis 2025, Indian DGCA to come under US FAA scanner this November

title: A tale of two deals: Trump's 'infinity' inspections meets Iran's 'not so fast'
content: the peace agreement purportedly pounded out during talks in the Alps has more holes than Swiss cheese . one side believes it has come away with a deal, the other is still dealing with the idea . the other side believes that the deal is going well . it turns out that the pact sounded suspiciously like a completed real-estate closing .
